ddls:
CREATE TABLE "provider_classifications" (
  "source_code" INTEGER /* Unique code for the healthcare provider classification */,
  "source_concept_id" INTEGER /* Concept ID from the source vocabulary */,
  "source_vocabulary_id" TEXT /* Vocabulary ID from the source vocabulary */,
  "source_vocabulary_version" TEXT /* Version of the source vocabulary */,
  "source_code_description" TEXT /* Description of the source code */,
  "target_concept_id" INTEGER /* Target concept ID from the target vocabulary */,
  "target_vocabulary_id" TEXT /* Vocabulary ID from the target vocabulary */,
  "target_vocabulary_version" TEXT /* Version of the target vocabulary */,
  "valid_start_date" TEXT /* Date when the classification became valid */,
  "valid_end_date" TEXT /* Date when the classification is no longer valid */,
  "invalid_reason" TEXT /* Reason for invalidation of the classification if applicable */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the classification */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the classification was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the classification */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the classification was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_provider_classifications_target_vocabulary_id FOREIGN KEY ("target_vocabulary_id") REFERENCES vocabularies ("vocabulary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_provider_classifications_source_vocabulary_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_vocabulary_id") REFERENCES vocabularies ("vocabulary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each healthcare provider */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the provider */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the provider */,
  "specialty" TEXT /* Specialty of the provider */,
  "license_number" TEXT /* License number of the provider */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the provider */,
  "provider_status" TEXT /* Status of the provider (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "provider_address" TEXT /* Address of the provider */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_providers_specialty FOREIGN KEY ("specialty") REFERENCES specialties ("specialty_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "specialties" (
  "specialty_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specialty */,
  "specialty_name" TEXT /* Name of the specialty */,
  "specialty_code" TEXT /* Code for the specialty */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the specialty */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("specialty_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vocabularies" (
  "vocabulary_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the vocabulary */,
  "vocabulary_name" TEXT /* Name of the vocabulary */,
  "vocabulary_version" TEXT /* Version of the vocabulary */,
  "vocabulary_publisher" TEXT /* Publisher of the vocabulary */,
  "vocabulary_publish_date" TEXT /* Date when the vocabulary was published */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the vocabulary and its purpose */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vocabulary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "invalidations" (
  "invalidation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each invalidation record */,
  "source_code" INTEGER /* Source code of the classification that has been invalidated */,
  "invalid_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the invalidation */,
  "invalidation_reason_code" TEXT /* Code for the reason of invalidation */,
  "invalidation_date" TEXT /* Date when the classification was invalidated */,
  "invalidated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who invalidated the classification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("invalidation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_invalidations_source_code FOREIGN KEY ("source_code") REFERENCES provider_classifications ("source_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, healthcare provider, data analyst) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken by the user (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "access_ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit record */,
  "source_code" INTEGER /* Source code of the classification that was changed */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who made the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., added, updated, deleted) */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_details" TEXT /* Details of the changes made to the classification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_source_code FOREIGN KEY ("source_code") REFERENCES provider_classifications ("source_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the notification is sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "date_sent" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, read) */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., new provider classification, updated classification) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
